channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: 'Gut feeling': Rising Socceroos star on why he moved to Norwich City
item.description: Rising Socceroos striker Mo Toure says a "gut feeling" was the reason behind his wildly successful move to the English Championship.

media:group.media:description: Mo Toure's move to Championship side Norwich City has more than paid off, with the Socceroos star scoring nine times in 11 league appearances.

item.title: Could Delta win Eurovision? The odds have soared in her favour
item.description: In a dazzling show of light and a powerful, tone-perfect delivery, Delta Goodrem may have just given Australia a real shot at winning Eurovision for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Annabel Bowles

media:group.media:description: Delta Goodrem has advanced to the 2026 Eurovision grand final.

item.title: Victorian ex-premier Andrews to be immortalised with $134k statue
item.description: The government says work is already underway to honour Daniel Andrews, who is Victoria's longest-serving Labor premier.

media:group.media:description: A statue of long-serving premier Daniel Andrews's likeness will be installed at Treasury Place.

item.title: Coalition tax plan could cost more than suggested in first few years
item.description: The Coalition's plan to index tax brackets could cost $35 billion in the first four years, more than has been suggested, and $44 billion in one year alone in 2035-36.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor has announced a plan to index tax brackets if elected.

item.title: Sick of maintaining your lawn? Transform it into a native garden
item.description: Many of us are looking to create a native garden as a lower maintenance, more sustainable and often cheaper alternative.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Scott

media:group.media:description: Think about a mix of medicinal, edible, toolmaking and visually appealing native plants instead of lawn.

item.title: ABC boss Hugh Marks flags TV cuts as broadcaster pivots to new platforms
item.description: The ABC's managing director says the public broadcaster's biggest challenge is "stopping things" as he seeks to grow audiences on new platforms without increasing staff.
item.dc:creator: David Taylor and Michael Janda

media:group.media:description: Hugh Marks says the ABC has regained the trust of its audience.

item.title: Students in hospital after MDMA 'overdose' at NSW school
item.description: Seven students at a Central Coast high school have been treated by paramedics following an incident involving MDMA.
item.dc:creator: Ben Clifford

media:group.media:description: Paramedics were called to Brisbane Water Secondary College following a drug-related emergency.

item.title: No decision on money for 'dead end' village after two-month highway closure
item.description: A small community's businesses say they will be forced to continue shedding staff and cutting days while the state government decides on financial relief.
item.dc:creator: Emily Middleton, Xanthe Gregory, and Lani Oataway

media:group.media:description: There is typically traffic through Little Hartley from the Great Western Highway.

item.title: Xi made a historical reference to give Trump a warning. What did it mean?
item.description: The US and China have been rivals for almost 80 years, but when the countries' leaders met in Beijing on Thursday, Xi Jinping made reference to a far more ancient feud.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Steedman

media:group.media:description: Xi Jinping referred to an ancient Greek war during his meeting with Donald Trump.

item.title: Ruling upheld after transgender woman excluded from female-only app
item.description: ​A landmark Federal Court ruling that the exclusion of a transgender woman from a female-only app constituted unlawful gender identity discrimination is upheld on appeal.
item.dc:creator: Jamie McKinnell

media:group.media:description: Sall Grover, centre, arrives at court ahead of the ruling.

item.title: Your questions about the hantavirus outbreak answered
item.description: As Australian travellers linked to the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak land back in Western Australia, we look at what we do and don't know about the virus.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Roberts

media:group.media:description: The mortality rate for people infected with the Andes strain of hantavirus is as high as one in two.

item.title: Police believe man found dead in Tarneit was kidnapped by armed intruders
item.description: A man's body was found near a children's playground in Melbourne's west after a violent home invasion in the city's north.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Brown

media:group.media:description: The man's body was found just metres from the playground at the Tarneit park.

item.title: Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers get another two weeks pay reprieve
item.description: Workers at Australia's only manganese smelter will be thrown an additional lifeline as the state and federal governments step in with a two-week pay offer. It comes days before a previous short-term deal was due to expire.
item.dc:creator: Sandy Powell

media:group.media:description: A delegation from the Australian Workers Union (AWU) travelled to Canberra last week to agitate for additional support for the smelter's workforce.

item.title: Surge with handball or control by foot: AFL challengers make choices in 2026
item.description: An early review of the AFL season so far points to a split between two distinct styles, with one side making a case for being flag favourites by showing it can deliver both.
item.dc:creator: Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson

media:group.media:description: Hawthorn may not lead the ladder, but they have the advantage of being strong at both surge and control styles of football.

item.title: Push to overturn SA fracking ban to be blocked by One Nation, Liberals
item.description: A controversial plan to end a ban on fracking in South Australia's South East two years earlier than planned, appears to be all but dead, with the Liberals and One Nation vowing to oppose legislation.

media:group.media:description: An onshore drilling operation in the Otway Basin.

item.title: Man and woman found dead at house north of Hobart
item.description: Tasmanian police are investigating the death of a man and woman, whose bodies have been found at a home in Campania, north of Hobart.

media:group.media:description: An ambulance leaves the property on Friday.

item.title: Eight people arrested at North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site, police say
item.description: Police say eight people have been arrested at the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site this morning.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Mason

media:group.media:description: A protester sits by a gate of the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

item.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers land in Perth to begin quarantine
item.description: A flight carrying six people who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship lands at an air force base in Perth.
item.dc:creator: Cason Ho and Andrea Mayes

media:group.media:description: The passengers, and the minibus driver, were wearing full-body protective gear.

item.title: AI to power medicines approvals but humans will still call the shots
item.description: This week’s budget revealed a number of ways the federal government is expected to use artificial intelligence to cut back on red tape. One expert says the benefits are "mostly good".
item.dc:creator: Cameron Wilson

media:group.media:description: The federal budget was filled with examples of ways the government hopes to use AI to save $10 billion in costs.

item.title: Sydney GP charged over alleged sexual assault of patients granted bail
item.description: Sydney GP Andrew Small is granted bail, after being charged with allegedly sexually assaulting multiple female patients at an inner-city medical centre.
item.dc:creator: Lia Harris

media:group.media:description: Dr Small worked at Waterloo Medical Centre.

item.title: Man with swastika tattooed on leg arrested in Alice Springs
item.description: A 25-year-old man has been arrested and charged in Alice Springs for displaying a tattoo of a swastika on his leg.

media:group.media:description: NT police have arrested a 25-year-old man for publicly displaying a prohibited Nazi symbol.

item.title: Carlton interim coach Fraser pulls out of running for full-time role
item.description: Josh Fraser will lead Carlton for the remainder of the season after Michael Voss's exit, but isn't prepared to do the job full-time just yet.

media:group.media:description: Josh Fraser has pulled out of the running to be Carlton's full-time senior coach.

item.title: Cattle industry loses court appeal over live export ban compensation
item.description: Australia's northern cattle industry says it is "deeply disappointed" by a Federal Court decision to dismiss its appeal over compensation for the 2011 live export ban.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brann and Joanna Prendergast

media:group.media:description: Australia's cattle industry has lost its appeal over the impact of the live export ban.

item.title: Cocaine hidden in imported fruit pulp worth estimated $4.6m, AFP says
item.description: Police say criminal syndicates are becoming more creative at concealing drugs within airport shipments.
item.dc:creator: Alex Sinnott

media:group.media:description: The shipment was identified by AFP officers as high-risk.

item.title: As the eldest daughter, I always knew I would care for my parents
item.description: As the eldest daughter in a Vietnamese family, it was an unspoken expectation I would be the one to care for my aging parents.

media:group.media:description: Cindy Mai knew from a young age she would care for her parents one day.
